name: TC-NFR-05: Delete User Account — Soft Delete Load Test
description: Validate that the PATCH /api/v1/users/{user_id} endpoint correctly processes 50 concurrent soft-delete requests (status update to deactivated) within the defined response time and success rate thresholds, and that deactivated users no longer appear in the active users listing.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/users/{user_id} PATCH endpoint must handle 50 simultaneous soft-delete requests with a maximum response time of 500 ms and a success rate of 98% or above. Following deactivation, affected users must not be retrievable via GET /users/active (data integrity constraint).
preconditions: At least 50 test users exist in the database with status: active.
Each test user has a unique user_id pre-loaded in a parameterisation CSV file.
Valid Bearer authorization tokens are provisioned for all 50 test users.
No test user has pending transactions that would block deactivation.
Application and database services are running and accessible.
Test environment is isolated from production traffic.

metrics.metric: Data Integrity — deactivated users absent from /users/active

notes: Duration of 300 s and ramp-up of 30 s are proposed defaults — adjust per test plan. Each VU must consume a unique user_id and token pair from CSV to prevent duplicate-deactivation conflicts. A dedicated post-test assertion step must query /users/active and confirm zero deactivated IDs are present (data integrity gate).
